1827	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput verifies that when the agent runner
1828	// returns an error, the error message appears in the JSON output Error field.
1829	// Exercises real git + RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for
1830	// the components under test.
1831	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput(t *testing.T) {
1832		workDir := t.TempDir()
1833	
1834		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1835			parent := os.Environ()
1836			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1837			for _, kv := range parent {
1838				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1839					continue
1840				}
1841				env = append(env, kv)
1842			}
1843			return env
1844		}
1845		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1846			t.Helper()
1847			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1848			cmd.Dir = workDir
1849			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1850			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1851			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1852			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1853		}
1854	
1855		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1856		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1857		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1858		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1859		runGit("add", ".")
1860		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1861	
1862		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1863			ID:        "my-bead",
1864			Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error message in output",
1865			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1866			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1867		})
1868		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1869		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1870		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1871	
1872		// Script harness: fail immediately on the first directive so the runner
1873		// returns (result, execErr) with no commits made. Mirrors the original
1874		// fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} with no commits.
1875		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1876		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1877			"fail-during 0\n",
1878		), 0o644))
1879	
1880		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1881		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1882		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1883	
1884		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1885		out, _ := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1886			"--from", baseSHA,
1887			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1888		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1889	
1890		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1891		// Agent runner's error message must appear in the JSON Error field.
1892		assert.Equal(t, "script harness: synthetic failure at directive 0", res.Error)
1893	}

1895	// TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure verifies that when HeadRev fails after the agent
1896	// runs, the outcome is "error" and the reason contains the original error message.
1897	// This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go lines 282-309.
1898	//
1899	// Exercises real git + RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for
1900	// the components under test. The script directive deletes the worktree's .git
1901	// pointer file as its only action, so the agent reports success but the
1902	// post-agent `git rev-parse HEAD` against the worktree fails for real.
1903	func TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1904		setup := func(t *testing.T) (workDir, baseSHA, directivePath string) {
1905			t.Helper()
1906			workDir = t.TempDir()
1907	
1908			scrubEnv := func() []string {
1909				parent := os.Environ()
1910				env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1911				for _, kv := range parent {
1912					if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1913						continue
1914					}
1915					env = append(env, kv)
1916				}
1917				return env
1918			}
1919			runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1920				t.Helper()
1921				cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1922				cmd.Dir = workDir
1923				cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1924				out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1925				require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1926				return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1927			}
1928	
1929			runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1930			runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1931			runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1932			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1933			runGit("add", ".")
1934			runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1935	
1936			seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1937				ID:        "my-bead",
1938				Title:     "Test execute-bead HeadRev failure",
1939				Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1940				IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1941			})
1942			runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1943			runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1944			baseSHA = runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1945	
1946			// Script directive: delete the worktree's .git pointer file. The agent
1947			// itself reports success (no error directive), but the post-agent
1948			// `git rev-parse HEAD` against the worktree must fail because there is
1949			// no longer a discoverable git directory at that path. Mirrors the
1950			// original fakeExecuteBeadGit{wtHeadRevErr: ...} contract using real
1951			// git instead of a hand-rolled error string.
1952			directivePath = filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1953			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1954				"delete-file .git\n",
1955			), 0o644))
1956			return
1957		}
1958	
1959		t.Run("json output", func(t *testing.T) {
1960			workDir, baseSHA, directivePath := setup(t)
1961	
1962			// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1963			f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1964			f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1965	
1966			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1967			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1968				"--from", baseSHA,
1969				"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1970			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1971			res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1972	
1973			assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1974			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1975			assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "rev-parse",
1976				"Reason must reflect the real git rev-parse failure on the corrupted worktree")
1977			assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1978		})
1979	
1980		t.Run("text output", func(t *testing.T) {
1981			workDir, baseSHA, directivePath := setup(t)
1982	
1983			f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1984			f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1985	
1986			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1987			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead",
1988				"my-bead",
1989				"--from", baseSHA,
1990				"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1991			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1992	
1993			assert.Contains(t, out, "outcome: error")
1994			assert.Contains(t, out, "rev-parse")
1995		})
1996	}

1998	// TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure verifies that when the
1999	// agent runner returns an error AND HeadRev fails on the worktree, both the
2000	// Error field (agent message) and the Reason field (rev error) are present in
2001	// the JSON output. This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go that
2002	// previously dropped the agent error message when revErr was non-nil.
2003	func TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
2004		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2005			mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
2006			wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("worktree HEAD unreadable"),
2007		}
2008		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent exploded"), result: nil}
2009		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2010	
2011		root := f.NewRootCommand()
2012		out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
2013		require.Error(t, cmdErr)
2014		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
2015	
2016		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
2017		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
2018		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
2019		assert.Equal(t, "agent exploded", res.Error,
2020			"agent error message must be preserved even when HeadRev also fails")
2021		assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "worktree HEAD unreadable",
2022			"Reason must reflect the HeadRev failure")
2023	}

2025	// TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID verifies that beadIDs with characters illegal
2026	// in git ref names are rejected with a clear error before any git or agent
2027	// operations are attempted.
2028	func TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID(t *testing.T) {
2029		invalidIDs := []string{
2030			"bead with spaces",
2031			"bead~1",
2032			"bead^1",
2033			"bead:name",
2034			"bead[0]",
2035		}
2036		for _, id := range invalidIDs {
2037			t.Run(id, func(t *testing.T) {
2038				git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111"}
2039				runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2040				f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2041	
2042				root := f.NewRootCommand()
2043				_, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", id)
2044				require.Error(t, cmdErr)
2045				assert.Contains(t, cmdErr.Error(), "invalid bead ID")
2046	
2047				// No git or agent operations should have been attempted.
2048				assert.Empty(t, git.addedWTs, "no worktree should be created for invalid bead ID")
2049			})
2050		}
2051	}

164	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
165	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
166	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
167	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
168	//
169	// Outcome rules:
170	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
171	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
172	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
173	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
174	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
175	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
176		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
177	
178		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
179		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
180		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
181		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
182			landing.Outcome = "error"
183			switch {
184			case res.Reason != "":
185				landing.Reason = res.Reason
186			case res.Error != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Error
188			default:
189				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
190			}
191			return landing, nil
192		}
193	
194		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
195		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
196			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
197			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
198				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
199			} else {
200				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
201			}
202			return landing, nil
203		}
204	
205		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
206		if res.ExitCode != 0 {
207			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
208			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
209				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
210			}
211			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
212			landing.PreserveRef = ref
213			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
214			return landing, nil
215		}
216	
217		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
218		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
219		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
220		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
221		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
222		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
223		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
224		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
225			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
226			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
227		)
228		if gateErr != nil {

22	
23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
51		mergeRev   string
52	}
53	
54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}

data.text:
Committed as `d96854fc`. Test passes and all AC items satisfied:

1. ✅ No references to `fakeExecuteBeadGit` or `fakeAgentRunner` in this test
2. ✅ Uses real git (scrubbed GIT_* env) + `agent.NewRunner` + script harness via `--harness script --model <directives>`
3. ✅ `go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure$' -count=1` green
4. ✅ Only `TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure` was modified; no other test in the file was touched
